explanation: The Button (BTN) always acts last on every post-flop street.
reaction_text: The Button (BTN) always acts last on every post-flop street.

prompt: You should play MORE hands from UTG than from the Button.
explanation: Incorrect. You should play FEWER hands from UTG because you have to act first and have many players behind.
goal: You should play MORE hands from UTG than from the Button.
reaction_text: Incorrect. You should play FEWER hands from UTG because you have to act first and have many players behind.

explanation: IP stands for In Position, meaning acting last relative to your opponent.
reaction_text: IP stands for In Position, meaning acting last relative to your opponent.

prompt: Why is acting last an advantage?
options: You can see opponent actions first
explanation: Seeing if your opponent checks or bets gives you critical information before you decide.

prompt: The Small Blind (SB) is always Out of Position (OOP) post-flop.
explanation: Yes, the SB is the first position to the left of the Button and acts first on every street post-flop.
